People cannot expect to do wrong and prosper; but rather to find that done to them which they did to others. How rarely do helpful truths reach the ears of those in high positions or with authority! Those who deceive others are preparing shame for their own faces. The prophet had a passionate love for God and for people's souls, deep concern for his glory and their salvation, and zeal against sin. The difficulties he faced did not drive him from his work. He had this strength; not from himself, but he was full of power by the Spirit of the Lord. Those who act honestly may act boldly. And those who come to hear the word of God must be willing to be told of their faults, take it kindly, and be thankful.

Zion’s walls owe no thanks to those that build them up with blood and wickedness. The sin of people does not work out God's righteousness. Even when people do something that is good in itself, but do it for selfish gain, it becomes an abomination both to God and people. Faith rests in the Lord as the soul’s foundation; presumption only leans upon the Lord as a prop, and would use him to serve a purpose. If people's having the Lord among them will not keep them from doing evil, it never can secure them from suffering evil for so doing. See the doom of wicked Jacob; Therefore Zion will be plowed as a field for your sake. This was exactly fulfilled at the destruction of Jerusalem by the Romans, and is so today. If sacred places are polluted by sin, they will be wasted and ruined by the judgments of God.
